growth_records.medium: BG11 supplemented with 10 mM sodium bicarbonate
growth_records.cite: Mendaña et al. Mutations in the circadian cycle drive adaptive plasticity in cyanobacteria. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2025). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2506928122. [PMC12435244]
growth_records.snippet: under conditions of high light intensity (HL, 983 µmol photons m−2 s−1), high temperature (HT, 41 °C), and high CO2 levels (HC, 3%), the generation time of C11 was 1.6 h
growth_records.notes: Generation (doubling) time of 1.6 h is directly reported for evolved strain C11 under HL(983 µmol photons m-2 s-1)/HT(41 °C)/HC(3% CO2), grown in BG11 + 10 mM NaHCO3 in a MC 1000-OD Multicultivator; growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/1.6 = 0.4332 h-1. Paper's own Methods define doubling time = Ln2/K' where K' is slope of Ln(OD720) in early exponential phase, so this is a reported/paper-calculated value we further convert to mu using the same standard formula. Text also states this represents a '654% increase compared to the wild-type (wt)' but the wt's absolute doubling time under this exact HL/HT/HC combination is not given as a number in the main text (only shown graphically in Fig. 1B), so no wt record is created to avoid inventing a value. Oxygen marked aerobic as photoautotrophic cyanobacterial culture (not explicitly labeled in text). pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: BG11 medium (supplemented with antibiotics and 10 mM bicarbonate when elevated CO2 used)
growth_records.cite: Mendaña et al. Mutations in the circadian cycle drive adaptive plasticity in cyanobacteria. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2025). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2506928122. [PMC12435244]
growth_records.snippet: S. elongatus UTEX 2973 (hereafter UTEX 2973), a S. elongatus strain that is 99.99% identical at the DNA level to PCC 7942, grows optimally at much higher light intensities, with doubling times as short as 2 h (25)
growth_records.notes: This is a background/introductory statement citing an external reference (ref. 25), not primary data generated in this study; medium, temperature and pH for the UTEX 2973 doubling time of 2 h are not given in this paper. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/2 = 0.3466 h-1 from the explicitly stated 2 h doubling time. Included at low confidence because the underlying experiment/conditions are not described here. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, medium]

up.method: photoautotrophic carbon source; rate not numerically reported

sec.method: literature-cited model constraint (ref 33) for engineered cscB+ strain; imposed in iJB792 M-model, dropped simulated growth 16% from 0.0539 to 0.044 h-1

growth_records.cite: Zuñiga et al. Synthetic microbial communities of heterotrophs and phototrophs facilitate sustainable growth. Nature Communications (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41467-020-17612-8. [PMC7393147]
growth_records.snippet: the growth rate dropped about 16% from 0.0539 h−1 to 0.044 h−1, similar to experimental observations (0.045 ± 0.07 h−1). ... the sucrose secretion rate of 0.182 mmol gDW−1 h−1 by an engineered S. elongatus strain (S. elongatus cscB+)
growth_records.notes: Experimental monoculture growth rate mu=0.045 +/- 0.07 h-1 (reported; note the reported SD 0.07 exceeds the mean, likely a typo in source). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.045 = 15.4 h (calculated). Model iJB792 predicted mu=0.044 h-1 with the sucrose secretion constraint of 0.182 mmol/gDW/h, and mu=0.0539 h-1 unconstrained. Sucrose secretion 0.182 mmol/gDW/h is a literature-cited constraint (ref 33), not measured in this study. S. elongatus is a prokaryotic oxygenic cyanobacterium. 35 C and pH 8.9 taken from the stated BG-11 inoculum conditions (1 g/L HEPES pH 8.9, 100 mM NaCl, 35 C).

growth_records.strain: PCC 7942 (Syn7942); WT and E. coli-LplA / MTS-LplA engineered variants

sec.compound: free fatty acids (incl. trans-linoleic acid C18:2n6t, TLA)
sec.units: increased vs WT (targeted FFA content, Fig. 8j; no numeric value in text)

growth_records.cite: Yang et al. Boosting energy metabolism and biosynthesis in diverse organisms by a common bacterial salvage lipoylation protein. Nat Commun (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-62638-5. [PMC12354856]
growth_records.snippet: the WT and mutants of S. elongatus were grown in BG11 medium at 28 C... P. tricornutum and S. elongatus are grown under photomixotrophic conditions with the addition of glycerol (100 mM) and glucose (5.5 mM) as sole organic carbon source, r
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote = cyanobacterium S. elongatus PCC 7942, grown photomixotrophically in BG11 + 5.5 mM glucose at 28 C. Growth rate reported ONLY graphically (Fig. 8b) as qualitatively higher in LplA/MTS-LplA vs WT; NO numeric growth rate, doubling time, uptake or secretion flux for the cyanobacterium is stated in the machine-readable text. The numeric specific growth rates of 0.025 and 0.022 h^-1 (MTS-LplA vs MTS-GFP, days 3-6) reported in this paper belong to CHO mammalian cells, NOT this prokaryote, so are excluded. BG11 base composition not itemized in text (standard recipe cited); only the 5.5 mM glucose supplement is explicitly stated, hence medium_composition_stated=false. FFA/TLA increase reported in Fig. 8j without numeric value. No arithmetic performed (no explicit input numbers available).

up.method: measured as net bicarbonate + dissolved-CO2 consumption (100 mM NaHCO3 added at day 0; remaining bicarbonate converted to CO2 and quantified)

sec.method: measured; text states WT sucrose was ~10-fold lower than Δxpk (1.3 g/L), i.e. ~0.13 g/L

growth_records.cite: Lu et al. An ATP-sensitive phosphoketolase regulates carbon fixation in cyanobacteria. Nature Metabolism (2023). DOI:10.1038/s42255-023-00831-w. [PMC10365998]
growth_records.snippet: At the end of the 4-d culture, the WT and Δxpk cultures respectively consumed 1 g l−1 and 1.6 g l−1 of carbon of bicarbonate and dissolved CO2... 1.3 g l−1 of sucrose was excreted from the Δxpk culture, which is tenfold higher than that fro
growth_records.notes: Wild-type S. elongatus PCC7942, high-density 12-h L/D batch over 4 days. Net carbon (CO2/bicarbonate) fixation = 1 g-C/L/4d (=0.25 g-C/L/d). WT sucrose secretion ~0.13 g/L/4d (computed as Δxpk 1.3 g/L ÷ 10, since text says WT is tenfold lower). Values are volumetric fluxes, not specific (per-gDW-per-h) rates: growth curves under 12-h L/D were similar WT vs Δxpk but no numeric µ reported. DCW calibration 384 mg/L per OD730=1. BG11 salt composition only cited (ref Rippka), not enumerated in text -> medium_composition_stated=false. Base growth medium for other experiments: BG11 pH8 + 50 mM NaHCO3, 30°C, 50 µmol m-2 s-1, 150 rpm.

up.method: measured as net bicarbonate + dissolved-CO2 consumption; ~60% increase over WT (also 63% more C fixed than WT at OD730=5 over 30 min)

sec.method: measured; synthesized in light, excreted in dark (tenfold higher than WT)
measured as a minor excreted fraction in carbon-partitioning (Fig 4h); no numeric value in text

growth_records.cite: Lu et al. An ATP-sensitive phosphoketolase regulates carbon fixation in cyanobacteria. Nature Metabolism (2023). DOI:10.1038/s42255-023-00831-w. [PMC10365998]
growth_records.snippet: the WT and Δxpk cultures respectively consumed 1 g l−1 and 1.6 g l−1 of carbon... 1.3 g l−1 of sucrose was excreted from the Δxpk culture, which is tenfold higher than that from the WT... the Δxpk strain fixed 63% more carbon than the WT st
growth_records.notes: Δxpk deletion strain, same high-density 12-h L/D 4-day batch. Net carbon (CO2/bicarbonate) fixation = 1.6 g-C/L/4d (=0.4 g-C/L/d), a ~60% increase over WT (abstract) / 63% more at OD730=5. Sucrose secretion 1.3 g/L/4d. Volumetric fluxes, not specific rates; no numeric µ. Deletion did not affect biomass, ATP level, or dark respiratory 13CO2 release vs WT.

growth_records.strain: PCC 7942 (WT, chromosomal reporter pkaiBC:eYFP-fsLVA at neutral site II)
growth_records.cite: Eremina et al. Environmental and molecular noise buffering by the cyanobacterial clock in individual cells. Nat Commun (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-58169-8. [PMC12000584]
growth_records.snippet: We validated the setup by observing cell growth and division rates comparable to previous reports on agarose pads (doubling time reported as 9.8 +/- 2.3 h ... at 25 umol m-2 s-1, compared to 9.9 +/- 3.6 h in our data at 20 umol m-2 s-1).
growth_records.notes: Reported single-cell doubling time in this study = 9.9 +/- 3.6 h at medium light (20 umol m-2 s-1). Specific growth rate mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/9.9 = 0.0700 h-1 (calculated from reported td). Temperature 30 C, 30% relative humidity, 25 umol m-2 s-1 cool-white light for bulk growth (Percival chambers); microfluidic single-cell imaging at 30 C. Medium named BG-11 M but full composition not enumerated in text (given by reference), so medium_composition_stated=false. The 9.8 +/- 2.3 h at 25 umol is a literature comparison value (not this study) and is not recorded as a separate record. Photoautotrophic: carbon from CO2 and energy from light, no quantified substrate uptake or product secretion fluxes reported. pH of BG-11 M not stated.

growth_records.cite: Wang, Renee Z. et al. Carbon isotope fractionation by an ancestral rubisco suggests that biological proxies for CO2 through geologic time should be reevaluated. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2300466120. [PMC10193938]
growth_records.snippet: ANC doubling times were roughly twice that of WT in the reference condition (20.8 +/- 1.2 vs. 12.0 +/- 1.4 h, respectively). ... reference condition (ambient pCO2 of 0.04% v/v, standard light flux (120 uE))
growth_records.notes: Reference/ambient condition: 120 umol photons m-2 s-1 (120 uE), 0.04% v/v pCO2, 30 C, BG-11 + 50 mM HEPES pH 8, bubbled ambient air. Reported doubling time td = 12.0 +/- 1.4 h (main text; SI Appendix Table S2). Growth rate calculated mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/12.0 = 0.0578 h-1. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates measured (photoautotroph; CO2 is sole C source but qCO2 not reported). Full BG-11 salt recipe not given in text (only HEPES 50 mM stated), so medium_composition_stated=false. Rubisco kinetics for this WT strain (Form 1B, from Syn. sp. PCC 6301 which shares RbcS/RbcL): Vc=9.78+/-0.48, KcAir=184.1 uM, Vc/KcAir=53.1, S_C/O=50.3+/-2.0 (from ref 47), eps_Rubisco(WT)=25.18+/-0.31 permil (not culture exchange fluxes).

growth_records.strain: PCC 7942, ANC (expressing reconstructed beta ancestral Form 1B rubisco; cbbL/cbbS replaced)
growth_records.cite: Wang, Renee Z. et al. Carbon isotope fractionation by an ancestral rubisco suggests that biological proxies for CO2 through geologic time should be reevaluated. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2300466120. [PMC10193938]
growth_records.snippet: ANC doubling times were roughly twice that of WT in the reference condition (20.8 +/- 1.2 vs. 12.0 +/- 1.4 h, respectively). This suggested that ANC's growth was limited by its ability to fix CO2 from ambient air.
growth_records.notes: Reference/ambient condition for the ancestral-rubisco strain: 120 uE, 0.04% v/v pCO2, 30 C, BG-11 + 50 mM HEPES pH 8. Reported td = 20.8 +/- 1.2 h (main text; SI Appendix Table S2). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/20.8 = 0.0333 h-1. Growth CO2-limited at ambient (rescued by high CO2). No uptake/secretion rates measured. Full BG-11 recipe not in text. ANC rubisco kinetics (this study): Vc lower than WT; eps_Rubisco(ANC) referenced but eps_Rubisco full value in SI.

growth_records.cite: Wang, Renee Z. et al. Carbon isotope fractionation by an ancestral rubisco suggests that biological proxies for CO2 through geologic time should be reevaluated. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2300466120. [PMC10193938]
growth_records.snippet: ameliorated by high pCO2, where doubling times for both strains were the same within uncertainty (WT 11.8 +/- 0.8 h; ANC 12.0 +/- 0.6 h). ... high CO2 (5% pCO2, 120 uE)
growth_records.notes: High-CO2 condition: 5% pCO2, 120 uE light, 30 C, BG-11 + 50 mM HEPES pH 8. Reported td = 11.8 +/- 0.8 h. mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/11.8 = 0.0587 h-1. No uptake/secretion rates. Full BG-11 recipe not stated in text.

growth_records.cite: Wang, Renee Z. et al. Carbon isotope fractionation by an ancestral rubisco suggests that biological proxies for CO2 through geologic time should be reevaluated. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2300466120. [PMC10193938]
growth_records.snippet: the highest ANC ep values were observed for cultures grown in high light, where growth was significantly slower than the WT (doubling time ~ 50 vs. 4 h, respectively) ... high light (0.04% pCO2, 500 uE)
growth_records.notes: High-light condition: 500 umol photons m-2 s-1, 0.04% v/v pCO2, 30 C, BG-11 + 50 mM HEPES pH 8, bubbled ambient air. Main-text doubling time for WT stated approximately as ~4 h (exact value in SI Appendix Table S2, not in fulltext). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/4.0 = 0.173 h-1 (approximate, since td is ~4 h). WT grew fastest under high light. No uptake/secretion rates measured. Confidence medium because td is an approximate (~) value in main text.

growth_records.strain: PCC 7942, ANC (reconstructed beta ancestral Form 1B rubisco)
growth_records.cite: Wang, Renee Z. et al. Carbon isotope fractionation by an ancestral rubisco suggests that biological proxies for CO2 through geologic time should be reevaluated. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2300466120. [PMC10193938]
growth_records.snippet: the highest ANC ep values were observed for cultures grown in high light, where growth was significantly slower than the WT (doubling time ~ 50 vs. 4 h, respectively). ... ANC growth is severely inhibited in high light.
growth_records.notes: High-light condition for ANC: 500 umol photons m-2 s-1, 0.04% v/v pCO2, 30 C, BG-11 + 50 mM HEPES pH 8. Main-text doubling time ~50 h (exact value in SI Appendix Table S2). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/50 = 0.0139 h-1 (approximate). ANC could not fix CO2 fast enough to match light-harvesting, severe photoinhibition. No uptake/secretion rates measured. Confidence medium because td is approximate (~) in main text.
